2606.24523.title: Poster: Exploring the Limits of Audio-Based Detection of Turkish Phone Call Scams
2606.24523.paper_summary: Scam phone calls exploit vulnerable communities worldwide, yet research on detection has focused almost exclusively on English and other high-resource languages. In low-resource settings such as Turkish, detection is especially difficult, as annotated data is scarce and technological defenses remain limited. This research investigates how large language models (LLMs) can support scam detection in Turkish by introducing the first public multi-modal dataset of 100 aligned audio-transcript pairs of scam and benign conversations. We evaluate seven LLMs spanning three model families: Gemini 2.5 (Flash, Flash-Lite, Pro), GPT-4o, and Qwen (Max, Plus, Turbo), under three input conditions: raw audio, automatic speech-to-text transcripts, and transcripts refined by a native speaker. Our results suggest that transcript-based inputs consistently outperform direct audio processing, while human-corrected and uncorrected transcripts perform comparably. By centering a low-resource language and real world threat, this work highlights the urgent need for culturally and linguistically inclusive AI safety research and more robust multi-modal systems for fraud prevention.
2606.24523.paper_summary_zh: 詐騙電話在全球範圍內利用脆弱社群，但有關檢測的研究幾乎完全集中在英語和其他高資源語言上。在土耳其等低資源環境中，檢測尤其困難，因為標註數據稀缺且技術防禦仍然有限。本研究探討大型語言模型（LLMs）如何支持土耳其語的詐騙檢測，並引入首個公開的100對詐騙和良性對話的多模態數據集。我們評估了七個LLM，涵蓋三個模型系列：Gemini 2.5（Flash、Flash-Lite、Pro）、GPT-4o和Qwen（Max、Plus、Turbo），在三種輸入條件下進行評估：原始音頻、自動語音轉文本轉錄，以及由母語者修正的轉錄。我們的結果表明，基於轉錄的輸入始終優於直接音頻處理，而人類修正和未修正的轉錄表現相當。通過聚焦於一種低資源語言和現實世界的威脅，本研究突顯了對文化和語言包容的AI安全研究以及更強大的多模態系統以防止詐騙的迫切需求。
2606.24523.author: Arda Eren et.al.
2606.24523.authors: Arda Eren, Micheal Cheung, Youqian Zhang, Grace Ngai, Eugene Yujun Fu
